categories.title: WORD ORIGINS & DEFINITIONS
categories.theme: Words and their meanings or origins — answer is the WORD or TERM being described

clues.clue: This part of speech modifies a verb, adjective, or another of its kind — 'hastily' is a classic example.
This everyday English word, meaning 'a commotion or fuss', likely blends Scottish and Irish dialect roots.
This verb meaning 'to rigidly categorize' derives from the small compartments once built into wooden writing desks.
This word describes coarsely abusive language, named after a London fish market notorious for foul-mouthed traders.
Cried from Edinburgh windows in the 18th century before slops were hurled into the street below, this warning word has since fallen out of use.

finals.title: WORD ORIGINS & DEFINITIONS
finals.clue: This obscure old Scottish word, meaning to chase girls around among the haystacks after dark, is a fine example of a term too vivid to forget — and too specific to survive.
